news.Title: Americans Are Warming Up to Robotaxis -- and That's Big News for Alphabet and Tesla
news.Content:
Would you feel anxious if a car with no driver showed up to take you to your destination? If so, you're not alone. A recent survey conducted by legal firm Murphy & Prachthauser found that 7 in 10 Americans would be at least somewhat apprehensive about riding in an autonomous rideshare vehicle.

However, the numbers look much better with people who have actually ridden in a self-driving vehicle. Although 85% said they were nervous initially, 64% felt comfortable during their most recent experience.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В»

The bottom line of this survey is that Americans appear to be warming up to robotaxis. And that's big news for two of the biggest self-driving car stocks -- Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) and Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) .

Alphabet's Waymo: The autonomous ride-hailing juggernaut

Alphabet's Waymo unit describes its technology as "the world's most experienced driver." The claim is based on the fact that the company's autonomous vehicles have logged over 20 million rides so far. This impressive number reflects Waymo's status as the world's first autonomous ride-hailing service.

Waymo's management probably isn't surprised by the results of the Murphy & Prachthauser survey, which show that Americans are more receptive to robotaxis than they've been in the past. Waymo boasts a 93% customer satisfaction rate.

The unit's safety record is even more impressive. Waymo's autonomous vehicles have 92% fewer serious injuries or worse crashes and 92% fewer pedestrian crashes with injuries than average human drivers over the same distance in the cities where the company operates. Insurance giant Swiss Re determined that Waymo is a significantly safer alternative than human drivers.

A recent funding round valued Waymo at $126 billion. That's only a fraction of Alphabet's market cap, which is roughly $3.5 trillion. However, the potential upside for Waymo is massive.

Tesla: Ready to scale

Tesla has targeted the robotaxi market for years, but has so far lagged behind Waymo. That could be about to change. The company now offers autonomous ride-hailing services in Austin, Texas, and plans to expand into more cities.

Lars Moravy, Vice President of Vehicle Engineering at Tesla, expressed his view in the quarterly conference call in January that Tesla is "the only company capable of scaling at the rate that is needed for the tsunami of autonomy that has come." Whether or not he's right, it's fair to say that Tesla's biggest advantage in the robotaxi market is its potential to scale.

Story Continues

Millions of Tesla vehicles equipped with its FSD (full self-driving) technology are already on the road. In theory, any of these cars could be used for autonomous ride-hailing. Tesla CEO Elon Musk said in his company's quarterly conference call in January.

There will also be an opportunity, something we have talked about for a long time, for existing owners of Teslas to add or subtract their cars to the fleet, kind of like how Airbnb (NASDAQ: ABNB) works, where you can add or subtract your house to the Airbnb inventory.

However, there's one factor that could be a two-edged sword for Tesla in its quest to dominate the robotaxi market. The company's technology relies solely on computer vision, whereas Waymo uses LiDAR sensors. A vision-only AI approach significantly lowers Tesla's costs. But the trade-off could be lower safety levels.

A new frontier

In the early days of the automobile, an expression became popular: "Get a horse!" The phrase underscored Americans' skepticism about the first cars. With nearly 300 million cars registered in the U.S. today, that view has obviously gone by the wayside.

We could be at a new frontier now with autonomous ride-hailing services. Look for Americans to become increasingly more comfortable with the technology. While the robotaxi era is in its infancy, it's here already.

Alphabet isn't just a search engine stock or cloud stock . Tesla is no longer just an electric car stock. They're poised to be the biggest winners in a new market that could make forward-thinking investors a boatload of money over time.

news.Title: Microsoft on track to invest $5.5 billion in Singapore by 2029, WSJ reports
news.Content:
April 1 (Reuters) - Microsoft вҖҢis on вҖҢtrack to вҖӢinvest $5.5 billion in cloud and вҖҢartificial вҒ intelligence infrastructure in вҒ Singapore through вҖӢ2029, вҖӢthe Wall вҖӢStreet вҖҢJournal reported on Wednesday.

Microsoft did not immediately вҖҢrespond вҖӢto вҖӢa вҖӢReuters вҖҢrequest for comment.

(Reporting вҖӢby вҖӢAnusha Shah вҖӢin вҖҢBengaluru; Editing вҖӢby Janane вҖӢVenkatraman)

news.Title: иЁҳжҶ¶й«”йЈҶжјІ жүӢж©ҹжҷ¶зүҮй–ӢгҖҢз Қе–®з¬¬1ж§ҚгҖҚпјҒе°Ҳе®¶жӣқйҖҷ2з”ўе“ҒдёӢдёҖжіўеҸ—е®і
news.Content:
иЁҳжҶ¶й«”е ұеғ№зӢӮйЈҶи§ёзҷје…Ёзҗғж¶ҲиІ»йӣ»еӯҗиӯҰе ұгҖӮзҫҺзі»еӨ–иіҮзӨәиӯҰпјҢеҸ—йҷҗж–јиЁҳжҶ¶й«”жҲҗжң¬жҝҖеўһпјҢ2026е№ҙе…Ёзҗғжҷәж…§еһӢжүӢж©ҹеҮәиІЁйҮҸйқўиҮЁйЎҜи‘—зҡ„дёӢдҝ®еЈ“еҠӣпјҢж•ҙй«”ж¶ҲиІ»йңҖжұӮе°ҮеҸ—еҲ°жҠ‘еҲ¶гҖӮиІЎз¶“е°Ҳе®¶йҳ®ж…•й©ҠеҲҶжһҗпјҢдёӯжқұжҲ°зҒ«е·Іе°Қе…ЁзҗғжҷҜж°Јз”ўз”ҹиІ йқўиЎқж“ҠпјҢиҖҢдё”жӢ–ж„Ҳд№…еҪұйҹҝж„ҲеӨ§пјҢжүӢж©ҹжҷ¶зүҮе·ІеҮәзҸҫгҖҢз Қе–®жҪ®гҖҚпјҢдёӢдёҖжіўжҳҜеҗҰе»¶зҮ’иҮіPCиҲҮNBпјҲзӯҶйӣ»пјүеёӮе ҙпјҹ

иЁҳжҶ¶й«”жјІеғ№еЈ“еҠӣ жүӢж©ҹйңҖжұӮиҪүејұ

йҳ®ж…•й©ҠеңЁиҮүжӣёзҷјж–ҮиЎЁзӨәпјҢиЁҳжҶ¶й«”еғ№ж јеӨ§жјІжӯЈзӣҙжҺҘиЎқж“Ҡж¶ҲиІ»з«ҜйңҖжұӮгҖӮзҫҺзі»еӨ–иіҮи§ҖеҜҹпјҢдёӯеңӢеӨ§йҷёеёӮе ҙжүӢж©ҹжҷ¶зүҮеә«еӯҳе·ІзҺҮе…Ҳдҝ®жӯЈпјҢеңЁжҲҗжң¬еўҠй«ҳжғ…жіҒдёӢпјҢжҷәж…§еһӢжүӢж©ҹйңҖжұӮйҖҗжӯҘеҸ—еҲ°еЈ“жҠ‘пјҢйҖЈеё¶еҪұйҹҝдёҠжёёдҫӣжҮүйҸҲгҖӮ

д»–жҢҮеҮәпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬иҒҜзҷјз§‘иҲҮй«ҳйҖҡе·Ій–Ӣе§ӢдёӢдҝ®4еҘҲзұіиЁӮе–®пјҢй җдј°жёӣе°‘зҙ„2иҗ¬иҮі3иҗ¬зүҮжҷ¶ең“пјҢзҙ„зӣёз•¶ж–јжҷ¶зүҮеҮәиІЁйҮҸжёӣе°‘1500иҗ¬иҮі2000иҗ¬йЎҶпјӣжӯӨеӨ–пјҢзӣёй—ңжё¬и©ҰдҫӣжҮүе•ҶеҰӮдә¬е…ғйӣ»д№ҹеҮәзҸҫйЎһдјјз Қе–®жғ…жіҒгҖӮдёҚйҒҺпјҢз”ұж–јAIиҲҮз¶ІйҖҡйңҖжұӮеј·еӢҒпјҢзӣёй—ңз”ўиғҪз©әзјәе·Іиҝ…йҖҹиў«еЎ«иЈңпјҢе°ҚеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҪұйҹҝжңүйҷҗгҖӮ

PCиҲҮNBйңҖжұӮжҳҜеҗҰеҸ—жіўеҸҠ еёӮе ҙдёӢдёҖжіўй—ңжіЁз„Ұй»һ

еӨ–иіҮеҗҢжӯҘдёӢдҝ®д»Ҡе№ҙе…ЁзҗғжүӢж©ҹеҮәиІЁйҮҸпјҢй җдј°е№ҙжёӣ13%пјҢе…¶дёӯAndroidйҷЈзҮҹиЎ°йҖҖе№…еәҰйҒ”15%пјҢиҮіж–јiPhoneпјҢеүҮеӣ ж–°е“ҒзҷјиЎЁжҷӮзЁӢиӘҝж•ҙпјҢйғЁеҲҶж©ҹеһӢеҸҜиғҪз”ұд»Ҡе№ҙз§ӢеӯЈе»¶еҫҢиҮіжҳҺе№ҙжҳҘеӯЈжҺЁеҮәпјҢеҪұйҹҝе…Ёе№ҙеҮәиІЁиЎЁзҸҫгҖӮ

йҳ®ж…•й©ҠжҢҮеҮәпјҢеӨ–иіҮе·Іе°ҮиҒҜзҷјз§‘жүӢж©ҹеҮәиІЁй җдј°дёӢдҝ®иҮіе№ҙжёӣ2жҲҗпјҢдёҰиӘҝйҷҚдә¬е…ғйӣ»з¬¬дәҢеӯЈзҮҹж”¶жҲҗй•·й җжңҹпјҢз”ұеҺҹе…ҲеӯЈеўһ15%дёӢдҝ®иҮі10%гҖӮд»–зӨәиӯҰпјҢжүӢж©ҹжҷ¶зүҮз Қе–®е·Із¶“еҮәзҸҫпјҢеҫҢзәҢжҳҜеҗҰж“ҙеҸҠPCиҲҮзӯҶйӣ»пјҲNBпјүеёӮе ҙпјҢеҖјеҫ—еҜҶеҲҮй—ңжіЁгҖӮ

д»–дёҰеј·иӘҝпјҢAIдјәжңҚеҷЁиҲҮйӣІз«ҜжңҚеӢҷжҘӯиҖ…е°ҚиЁҳжҶ¶й«”еғ№ж јдёҠжјІжүҝеҸ—еҠӣијғй«ҳпјҢдҪҶж¶ҲиІ»з«Ҝе°Қеғ№ж јж•Ҹж„ҹеәҰжҳҺйЎҜдёҚеҗҢгҖӮеңЁдёӯжқұжҲ°дәӢиҲҮйҖҡиҶЁеЈ“еҠӣдәӨз№”дёӢпјҢж•ҙй«”зү©еғ№дёҠжјІи¶ЁеӢўе·ІйҖҗжӯҘж“ҙж•ЈпјҢеёӮе ҙдёҚе®ңйҒҺеәҰжңҹеҫ…fedйҷҚжҒҜпјҢгҖҢдёҚеҚҮжҒҜгҖҚжҒҗе·ІжҳҜе°ҚиӮЎеёӮжңҖжңүеҲ©зҡ„жғ…еўғгҖӮ

news.Title: дәһиӮЎе ұеҫ©жҖ§еҸҚеҪҲпјҒеүөдёҖе№ҙжңҖзҢӣжјІе№… еҸ°з©Қйӣ»гҖҒдёүжҳҹгҖҒSKжө·еҠӣеЈ«й ҳи»ҚиЎқйӢ’
news.Content:
з”ұж–јеёӮе ҙжЁӮи§Җй җжңҹдјҠжң—жҲ°зҲӯеҸҜиғҪеңЁиҝ‘жңҹзөҗжқҹпјҢдәһиӮЎиҝҪйҡЁзҫҺиӮЎжјІеӢўпјҢеңЁе‘Ёдёү (1 ж—Ҙ) еүөдёӢдёҖе№ҙдҫҶжңҖеӨ§е–®ж—ҘжјІе№…гҖӮ

MSCI дәһеӨӘжҢҮж•ёеңЁ 4 жңҲз¬¬дёҖеҖӢдәӨжҳ“ж—Ҙеұ•й–Ӣеј·еҠӣеҸҚеҪҲгҖҒдёҖеәҰйЈҶеҚҮ 5.1%пјҢеүөдёӢеҺ»е№ҙ 4 жңҲ 10 ж—Ҙд»ҘдҫҶжңҖеӨ§жјІе№…гҖӮеҸ°ж—Ҙйҹ“иӮЎеёӮй ҳжјІпјҢеҚ—йҹ“ Kospi жҢҮж•ёдёҖйҰ¬з•¶е…ҲпјҢзөӮе ҙеӨ§жјІ 8.4%пјҢж—ҘиӮЎж—Ҙз¶“ 225 жҢҮж•ёйЈҶеҚҮ 2675 й»һжҲ– 5.2%пјҢеҸ°зҒЈеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёд№ҹеӨ§жјІ 1451 й»һгҖҒзӣёз•¶ж–ј 4.58%гҖӮ

з§‘жҠҖж¬ҠеҖјиӮЎжӯёйҡҠпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ» (2330-TW) зөӮе ҙдёҠжјІ 5.4%пјҢдёүжҳҹйӣ»еӯҗзӢӮйЈҶ 13.4%пјҢSK жө·еҠӣеЈ«еј·еҪҲ 10.66%пјҢдёүиҖ…еҗҢзӮәжҝҖеӢөдәһиӮЎзҡ„жңҖеӨ§еҠҹиҮЈгҖӮ

йҹ“иҒҜзӨҫе ұе°ҺпјҢеҚ—йҹ“дәӨжҳ“жүҖ (KRX) ж–јй–ӢзӣӨзҙ„дёғеҲҶйҗҳеҫҢпјҢдҫҝе•ҹеӢ•зӮәжңҹ 5 еҲҶйҗҳзҡ„гҖҢиІ· / иіЈжҡ«еҒңж©ҹеҲ¶гҖҚ(sidecar)пјҢжҡ«жҷӮеҒңжӯў KOSPI жңҹиІЁзҡ„зЁӢејҸдәӨжҳ“иІ·е–®гҖӮйҖҡеёёеңЁжңҹиІЁжҢҮж•ёжіўеӢ•йҒ”еҲ° 5% д»ҘдёҠжҷӮпјҢе°ұжңғеңЁзҸҫиІЁеёӮе ҙи§ёзҷјгҖҢиІ· / иіЈжҡ«еҒңж©ҹеҲ¶гҖҚпјҢзӣ®зҡ„еңЁз·©е’ҢеёӮе ҙжіўеӢ•гҖӮ

еёӮе ҙйўЁйҡӘиғғеҸЈеҘҪиҪүпјҢдё»еӣ жҳҜзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®иЎЁзӨәпјҢзҫҺеңӢе°ҮеңЁгҖҢе…©гҖҒдёүе‘Ёе…§гҖҚзөҗжқҹиҲҮдјҠжң—зҡ„жҲ°зҲӯпјҢдёҰжҡ—зӨәдјҠжң—д»ҚжңүеҸҜиғҪеңЁжӯӨжңҹйҷҗе…§иҲҮзҫҺеңӢйҒ”жҲҗеҚ”иӯ°гҖӮ

зҷҪе®®е·Іе®ЈеёғпјҢе·қжҷ®й җе®ҡзҫҺжқұе‘Ёдёүжҷҡй–“ 9 й»һ (еҸ°зҒЈе‘ЁеӣӣдёҠеҚҲ 9 й»һ) зҷјиЎЁжңүй—ңдјҠжң—жҲ°зҲӯзҡ„е…ЁеңӢжј”иӘӘпјҢеҗ„з•Ңй«ҳеәҰй—ңжіЁзҫҺдјҠжҲ°зҲӯжҳҜеҗҰзңҹеҰӮе·қжҷ®жүҖиЁҖеҚіе°ҮзөҗжқҹгҖӮ

еӨ–еӘ’еҗҢж—ҘйӮ„жҠ«йңІпјҢдјҠжң—зёҪзөұиЈҙжҫӨж–Ҝеҹәе®ү (Masoud Pezeshkian) е·Із¶“иЎЁзӨәпјҢеҫ·й»‘иҳӯж“ҒжңүгҖҢеҝ…иҰҒзҡ„ж„Ҹеҝ—гҖҚзөҗжқҹиҲҮзҫҺеңӢе’Ңд»ҘиүІеҲ—д№Ӣй–“йҖҷе ҙжҲ°зҲӯпјҢеҗҢжҷӮе°ӢжұӮйҖҷе ҙиЎқзӘҒдёҚжңғйҮҚжј”зҡ„дҝқйҡңж©ҹеҲ¶гҖӮ

KCM Trade йҰ–еёӯеёӮе ҙеҲҶжһҗеё« Tim Waterer иӘӘпјҡгҖҢжҠ•иіҮдәәзңӢеҘҪиЎқзӘҒиғҪеңЁж•ёе‘ЁиҖҢйқһж•ёжңҲе…§и§ЈжұәпјҢйҖҷи®“еёӮе ҙд»Ҡж—ҘйҖІе…ҘиІ·и¶…жЁЎејҸгҖӮдҪҶз”ұж–јжІ№еғ№д»Қз¶ӯжҢҒеңЁдёүдҪҚж•ёй«ҳжӘ”пјҢдё”й—ңж–јиҚ·е§ҶиҢІжө·еіҪе‘ҪйҒӢзҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜд»ҚеҘҪеЈһеҸғеҚҠпјҢе°ҚеёӮе ҙиҖҢиЁҖпјҢзҸҫеңЁйӮ„зЁұдёҚдёҠжҳҜдёҖеёҶйўЁй ҶгҖӮгҖҚ

еҖјеҫ—жіЁж„Ҹзҡ„жҳҜпјҢеҚідҪҝ MSCI дәһеӨӘжҢҮж•ёе‘Ёдёүеј·еҠӣеҸҚеҪҲпјҢдҪҶиҲҮ 2 жңҲзҡ„й«ҳй»һзӣёжҜ”д»ҚдёӢи·Ңзҙ„ 9%пјҢйЎҜзӨәеёӮе ҙе°Қж–јжІ№еғ№дёӢи·Ңзҡ„йҖҹеәҰгҖҒд»ҘеҸҠе·қжҷ®дҝқиӯүзҡ„еҸҜдҝЎеәҰпјҢд»Қжү“дёҠе•ҸиҷҹгҖӮ

news.Title: еӢһйҮ‘еұҖпјҡеҸ°иӮЎ3жңҲи·ҢйҖҫ10%гҖҖйңҮзӣӘдёӯжӘўиҰ–дҪҺдј°йғЁдҪҚ
news.Content:
пјҲдёӯеӨ®зӨҫиЁҳиҖ…ејөйӣ„йўЁеҸ°еҢ—2026е№ҙ04жңҲ1ж—Ҙйӣ»пјүеӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘д»Ҡе№ҙеүҚ2еҖӢжңҲж”¶зӣҠж•ёзӮәж–°еҸ°е№Ј8131е„„е…ғгҖӮеӢһйҮ‘еұҖиЎЁзӨәпјҢз”ұж–ј3жңҲеңӢйҡӣеёӮе ҙдёӢж®әеҡҙйҮҚпјҢз”ҡиҮіи®“1гҖҒ2жңҲзҡ„зҚІеҲ©еӣһеҗҗпјҢйӣ–з„¶еҸ°иӮЎзЁҚдҪідҪҶд»ҚдёӢи·ҢйҖҫ10%пјӣжңғеңЁеёӮе ҙйңҮзӣӘдёӯжӘўиҰ–жҳҜеҗҰжңүиў«дҪҺдј°зҡ„йғЁдҪҚгҖӮ

еӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘йҒӢз”ЁеұҖд»ҠеӨ©е…¬еёғж•ҙй«”еӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘жңҖж–°зёҫж•ҲпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙжҲӘиҮі2жңҲеә•пјҢж•ҙй«”еӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘иҰҸжЁЎзӮә8е…Ҷ854е„„е…ғпјҢж”¶зӣҠж•ёзӮә8131е„„е…ғпјҢж”¶зӣҠзҺҮзӮә10.83%пјҢ2жңҲе–®жңҲж”¶зӣҠзӮә4184е„„е…ғгҖӮ

ж №ж“ҡзөұиЁҲпјҢжӯ·е№ҙе–®жңҲеӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘ж”¶зӣҠпјҢжң¬жңҹзӮәе–®жңҲжңҖй«ҳпјӣе…¶ж¬ЎзӮәд»Ҡе№ҙ1жңҲзҡ„3947е„„е…ғгҖҒ2025е№ҙ10жңҲ3006е„„е…ғгҖӮ

еӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘йҒӢз”ЁеұҖеүҜеұҖй•·еҠүйә—иҢ№дёӢеҚҲеңЁиЁҳиҖ…жңғдёӯиЎЁзӨәпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙйҖЈзәҢ1гҖҒ2жңҲйғҪеҲ·ж–°е–®жңҲж”¶зӣҠжӯ·еҸІж–°й«ҳпјҢдёҚйҒҺзӣ®еүҚи§ҖеҜҹ3жңҲд»Ҫзҡ„йҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙпјҢдёҚи«–жҳҜйҮ‘иһҚеёӮе ҙгҖҒеҢҜзҺҮжҲ–жІ№еғ№зӯүпјҢйңҮзӣӘйқһеёёеҠҮзғҲгҖӮ

еҠүйә—иҢ№жҢҮеҮәпјҢзӣ®еүҚзңӢ3жңҲйҮ‘иһҚжҢҮжЁҷпјҢе…Ёдё–з•Ңзҡ„и·Ңе№…йғҪеҫҲеӨ§пјҢ3жңҲйҮ‘иһҚзӣёй—ңжҢҮжЁҷиЎЁзҸҫдёҚдҪіпјҢеҸ°иӮЎи·Ңзҙ„10.42%пјҢеңӢйҡӣеёӮе ҙдҫҶзңӢпјҢжҳҜе°Үд»Ҡе№ҙ1гҖҒ2жңҲж”¶зӣҠгҖҢеҖ’еҗҗеӣһеҺ»йӮ„дёҚеӨ гҖҚпјӣеҸ°иӮЎжҳҜзӣ®еүҚе…ЁзҗғеёӮе ҙдёӯзӣёе°ҚијғеҘҪзҡ„гҖӮ

з”ұж–јдёӯжқұжҲ°зҲӯпјҢеҗ„з•ҢжҶӮеҝғжІ№еғ№жіўеӢ•жңғеҪұйҹҝеёӮе ҙгҖӮеҠүйә—иҢ№и§ЈйҮӢпјҢеӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘жҳҜеұ¬ж–јй•·жңҹжҠ•иіҮпјҢдёҚеғҸзҹӯжңҹйҖІеҮәе…ҘеёӮе ҙпјҢеӣ жӯӨжҳҜзңӢж•ҙй«”з”ўжҘӯзҡ„и¶ЁеӢўпјӣд»ҘAIз”ўжҘӯзӮәдҫӢпјҢеңЁAIзҡ„и¶ЁеӢўдёӢжҳҜеҗҢжЁЈзҡ„ж–№еҗ‘пјҢйңҮзӣӘжңғи®ҠжҲҗжҳҜдёҖеҖӢж©ҹжңғпјҢжӘўиҰ–жҳҜеҗҰжңүиў«дҪҺдј°зҡ„йғЁдҪҚгҖӮ

еҠүйә—иҢ№иЈңе……пјҢжІ№еғ№е•ҸйЎҢеј•зҷјзҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҳҜйҖҡиҶЁпјҢд№ҹе°ұжҳҜеӣ зӮәжІ№еғ№дёҠжјІдҫөиқ•зҚІеҲ©пјҢдёҖжЁЈзҡ„з”ўе“ҒиҰҒиҠұжӣҙеӨҡйҢўиІ·пјҢеҰӮжһңеҸӘжҳҜдёҖйҷЈеӯҗйӮ„еҘҪпјҢжҜ”ијғйңҖиҰҒж“”еҝғжңғдёҚжңғи®ҠжҲҗй•·жңҹзҡ„зӢҖж…Ӣпјӣе°ұзӣ®еүҚзңӢдҫҶжҮүи©ІеҸӘжҳҜзҹӯжңҹйңҮзӣӘпјҢжңғжҢҒзәҢй—ңжіЁгҖӮ

еӢһйҮ‘еұҖзөұиЁҲпјҢеӢһеӢ•еҹәйҮ‘еҸҠеңӢдҝқеҹәйҮ‘иҝ‘10е№ҙеӨҡпјҲ105е№ҙиҮід»Ҡе№ҙ2жңҲпјүе№іеқҮж”¶зӣҠзҺҮеҲҶеҲҘзӮә10.04%еҸҠ10.33%пјҢиҝ‘5е№ҙеӨҡе№іеқҮж”¶зӣҠзҺҮеҲҶеҲҘзӮә12.61%еҸҠ12.68%пјҢжҠ•иіҮзёҫж•Ҳз©©еҒҘгҖӮ

news.Title: еҸ°з©Қйӣ»её¶й ӯжҡҙиЎқжјІ95е…ғпјҒ еҸ°иӮЎж”¶жјІ1451й»һжҲҗеҸІдёҠз¬¬2еӨ§

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: еҸ°иӮЎж»ҝиЎҖеҫ©жҙ»з«ҷеӣһ3иҗ¬3гҖҒжҠ•дҝЎзӢӮжҺғ101е„„ дёүеӨ§жі•дәәиІ·и¶…549.82е„„е…ғ
news.Content:
зҫҺдјҠе’Ңи«ҮйңІжӣҷе…үпјҢжҝҖеӢөеҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡ (1) ж—Ҙеј·еӢўеҸҚеҪҲпјҢиЁҳжҶ¶й«”гҖҒзҹҪжҷәиІЎгҖҒPCB еҸҠдҪҺи»ҢиЎӣжҳҹзӯүж—ҸзҫӨе…ЁйқўеҷҙжјІпјҢзөӮе ҙеӨ§жјІ 1451.83 й»һжҲ– 4.58%пјҢеүөеҸ°иӮЎз¬¬дәҢеӨ§жјІй»һпјҢд»Ҙ 33174.82 й»һдҪңж”¶пјҢдёҖиҲүз«ҷеӣһ 33000 й»һй—ңеҚЎпјҢеҗҢжӯҘж”¶еҫ© 5 ж—Ҙз·ҡиҲҮеӯЈз·ҡпјҢжҲҗдәӨйҮҸ 6161.23 е„„е…ғгҖӮ пјӣдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶… 549.82 е„„е…ғгҖӮ

и§ҖеҜҹдёүеӨ§жі•дәәд»ҠеӨ©зұҢзўјеӢ•еҗ‘пјҢеӨ–иіҮиІ·и¶… 232.51 е„„пјӣиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶиІ·и¶… 216.2 е„„е…ғпјӣжҠ•дҝЎиІ·и¶… 101.11 е„„е…ғпјӣдёүеӨ§жі•дәәеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶… 549.82 е„„е…ғгҖӮ

news.Title: гҖҗFollowжі•дәәгҖ‘йқўжқҝдёҚеҗҢиӘҝпјҒеӨ–иіҮжЈ„зҫӨеүөжӢұеҸӢйҒ” дё»еӢ•ејҸETF00981AжҲҗеҜөе…’жҺғиІЁ6.6иҗ¬ејө
news.Content:
пјҲдёӢеҚҲ5жҷӮ16еҲҶжӣҙж–°еӨ–иіҮиІ·иіЈи¶…еҖӢиӮЎпјү

еҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ1пјүж—ҘеӢҒжҸҡ1451.83й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ33174.82й»һгҖӮдёүеӨ§жі•дәәиҒҜиўӮиІ·и¶…549.82е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮеӣһиЈң232.51е„„е…ғпјҢзөӮзөҗйҖЈзәҢ4иіЈпјҢдё»иҰҒиҪүиІ·и¶…дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·пјҲ00919пјү6.6иҗ¬ејөпјӣиЁҳжҶ¶й«”иӮЎиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»пјҲ2344пјүе’ҢеҠӣз©Қйӣ»пјҲ6770пјүеүҮйҖҶеӢўйҒӯиӘҝзҜҖгҖӮеҲҶжһҗеё«иЎЁзӨәпјҢеӨ–иіҮиІ·и¶…йҮ‘йЎҚдёҚз®—еӨҡпјҢеғ…иғҪзЁұзӮәгҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚпјҢе…¶д»ҚеңЁи©•дј°еҫҢзәҢеұҖеӢўзҡ„зҷјеұ•пјҢе°ҡжңӘеҮәзҸҫжҳҺзўәзҡ„й•·з·ҡеӣһиЈңиЁҠиҷҹгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮд»Ҡж—ҘиІ·и¶…еүҚдёүеӨ§пјҡдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·гҖҒеҸ°ж–°ж–°е…үйҮ‘гҖҒеҸӢйҒ”

дё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·пјҲ00919пјүд»Ҡе№ҙдҫҶиЎЁзҸҫжҗ¶зңјпјҢеҗёеј•иіҮйҮ‘еӨ§иҲүж№§е…ҘпјҢеӨ–иіҮд»ҠзөӮзөҗйҖЈ3иіЈпјҢиҪүиІ·и¶…6иҗ¬6,509ејөеұ…еҸ°иӮЎиІ·и¶…д№ӢеҶ пјӣдё»еӢ•еҫ©иҸҜжңӘдҫҶ50пјҲ00991Aпјүд№ҹжңү3иҗ¬904ејөиІ·и¶…гҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮдё»иҰҒиІ·и¶…йӢјйҗөеЎ‘еҢ–иӮЎпјҢеҢ…жӢ¬дёӯйӢјпјҲ2002пјү1иҗ¬8,820ејөгҖҒеҚ—дәһпјҲ1303пјү1иҗ¬6,451ејөд»ҘеҸҠеҸ°еҢ–пјҲ1326пјү9,229ејөгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮд»Ҡж—ҘиіЈи¶…еүҚдёүеӨ§пјҡиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»гҖҒеҠӣз©Қйӣ»гҖҒзҫӨеүө

жҶ¶й«”ж—ҸзҫӨд»Ҡж—ҘйҡЁи‘—зҫҺе…үжӯўи·ҢеҸҚеҪҲпјҢз„¶иҖҢпјҢеӨ–иіҮйҖҶеӢўз ҚиҸҜйӮҰйӣ»пјҲ2344пјү3иҗ¬628ејөпјҢд»ҘеҸҠеҠӣз©Қйӣ»пјҲ6770пјү2иҗ¬6,479ејөгҖӮзӣӨйқўдә®зңјзҡ„йқўжқҝж—ҸзҫӨеүҮжҳҜдёҚеҗҢиӘҝпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҮәи„«зҫӨеүөпјҲ3481пјү1иҗ¬3,926ејөпјҢиІ·и¶…еҸӢйҒ”пјҲ2409пјү3иҗ¬3,792ејөгҖӮ

еӨ–иіҮеғ…иІ·233е„„е…ғ еҲҶжһҗеё«пјҡгҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚжңӘзҝ»еӨҡ

ж №ж“ҡиӯүдәӨжүҖе…¬еёғзұҢзўјеӢ•еҗ‘пјҢдёүеӨ§жі•дәәд»Ҡж—ҘеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶…549.82е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯеӨ–иіҮиІ·и¶…232.51е„„е…ғпјҢзөӮзөҗйҖЈзәҢ4иіЈгҖӮеӨ–иіҮеүҚ4еҖӢдәӨжҳ“ж—Ҙе…ұиӘҝзҜҖеҸ°иӮЎйҒ”1886.58е„„е…ғгҖӮ

жҠ•дҝЎзәҢз«ҷиІ·ж–№пјҢиІ·и¶…101.1иҗ¬е…ғгҖӮиҮӘзҮҹе•ҶеҗҲиЁҲиІ·и¶…216.19е„„е…ғпјҢе…¶дёӯиҮӘиЎҢиІ·иіЈйғЁеҲҶиІ·и¶…67.6е„„е…ғпјҢйҒҝйҡӘж–№йқўиІ·и¶…148.58е„„е…ғгҖӮ

иӯүжңҹйӣҷз…§еҲҶжһҗеё«зҝҒеҒүжҚ·еҸ—иЁӘиЎЁзӨәпјҢеҸ°иӮЎд»Ҡж—ҘеӨ§жјІйҖҫеҚғй»һпјҢеҰӮжӯӨйү…еӨ§жјІе№…пјҢеӨ–иіҮз…§зҗҶжҮүиІ·и¶…дёғгҖҒе…«зҷҫе„„е…ғпјҢдҪҶд»Ҡж—ҘеӣһиЈңзҡ„йҮҸдёҚз®—еӨҡпјҢйЎҜзӨәе…¶е°ҚеҸ°иӮЎзҡ„еҫҢзәҢиЎЁзҸҫд»Қеӯҳжңүз–‘ж…®пјҢеғ…иғҪиҰ–зӮәзҹӯжңҹзҡ„гҖҢиё©еүҺи»ҠгҖҚпјҢиҖҢйқһе…ЁйқўзңӢеҘҪеӣһжӯёгҖӮ

зҝҒеҒүжҚ·иӘӘжҳҺпјҢеӨ–иіҮжҢҒи§Җжңӣж…ӢеәҰзҡ„дё»иҰҒеҺҹеӣ еңЁж–јиҚ·е§ҶиҢІжө·еіҪе°ҒйҺ–жҷӮй–“зҡ„й•·зҹӯпјҢе°Қе…Ёзҗғз¶“жҝҹжҢҒзәҢз”ўз”ҹеҪұйҹҝгҖӮжӯӨеӨ–пјҢж №ж“ҡзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®жңҖж–°зҡ„иӘӘжі•пјҢжҲ°зҲӯеҸҜиғҪйӮ„йңҖиҮіе°‘е…©иҮідёүе‘ЁжүҚиғҪзөҗжқҹпјҢйҖҷдҪҝеҫ—еӨ–иіҮеңЁдҪңйӮҸијҜдёҠпјҢзӣ®еүҚеңЁе…ЁзҗғиіҮз”ўйғЁдҪҚд»ҚеӮҫеҗ‘д»ҘиіЈи¶…зӮәдё»пјҢе°ҚеҸ°иӮЎзҡ„иІ·зӣӨеҠӣйҒ“еӣ жӯӨеҸ—йҷҗгҖӮ

news.Title: 2 No-Brainer Software Stocks to Buy Right Now
news.Content: The arrival of artificial intelligence (AI) propelled many technology stocks skyward, but that changed in 2026. Wall Street realized AI could make some software businesses obsolete, leading to a sell-off that contributed to the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite officially falling into correction territory . Still, the situation creates opportunities for the industrious investor to scoop up shares ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў ServiceNow reported strong Q4 2025 sales growth of 21% year-over-year to $3.6 billion, with subscription sales representing stable recurring revenue.
вҖў ServiceNow expects Q1 2026 subscription sales to rise 22% year-over-year to around $3.7 billion.
вҖў Salesforce posted record revenue of $11.2 billion for its fiscal Q4 2026, with its Agentforce AI solutions showing strong adoption.
вҖў Salesforce's Agentforce Q4 annual recurring revenue rose 169% year-over-year to $800 million.
вҖў Both ServiceNow and Salesforce stocks have experienced significant price drops in 2026, presenting potential buying opportunities.
news.Keywords: AI, software platform, recurring revenue, valuation, growth stock

news.Title: Better Artificial Intelligence Stock: Palantir vs. Oracle
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) industry experienced upheaval in 2026. Wall Street soured on many AI stocks, but this creates opportunities for investors looking at the long term. Two AI businesses to consider investing in are Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) and Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) . The former gained prominence providing data analytics to government intelligence agencies, while the latter began ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Palantir's Q4 2025 revenue increased by 70% year-over-year to $1.4 billion, driven by its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP).
вҖў Oracle's fiscal Q3 2026 revenue rose 22% year-over-year to $17.2 billion, with remaining performance obligations (RPO) totaling $553 billion, up 325% YoY.
вҖў Palantir has no debt and total assets of $8.9 billion against $1.4 billion in liabilities at the end of 2025.
вҖў Oracle has accumulated over $130 billion in debt to fund its cloud expansion.
вҖў Despite Palantir's high P/S ratio, its stronger sales growth and financial health make it a preferred AI investment over Oracle.
news.Keywords: AI platform, cloud computing, revenue growth, valuation, balance sheet

news.Title: гҖҗHotеҸ°иӮЎгҖ‘еӨ§зӣӨзӢӮжјІ1416й»һж•ЈжҲ¶еӢ•еҪҲдёҚеҫ—пјҹз¶ІжҶӮгҖҢе·қжҷ®пјҡж„ҡдәәзҜҖеҝ«жЁӮгҖҚ  е°Ҳе®¶пјҡдҝқз•ҷ3жҲҗзҸҫйҮ‘зӮәе®ң
news.Content:
д»Ҡж—ҘзӨҫзҫӨеҸ°иӮЎжңҖHotиӯ°йЎҢ

гҖҢе·қжҷ®пјҡж„ҡдәәзҜҖеҝ«жЁӮгҖҚгҖҢеҺ»е№ҙжё…жҳҺзҜҖи®ҠзӣӨпјҢд»Ҡе№ҙж„ҡдәәзҜҖпјҢйғҪжҳҜзҝ»иә«зҡ„ж©ҹжңғгҖҚгҖҢй–ӢзӣӨеҷҙеҚғй»һ ж”¶зӣӨж„ҡдәәзҜҖгҖҚгҖҢеӨ§е®¶йғҪи®ҠиҒ°жҳҺдәҶе—ҺпјҹйҮҸйҖҷйәје°‘пјҢйғҪжІ’дәәиІ·дәҶе–”гҖҚгҖҢеӨ§зӣӨжҖҺйәји·ҹжӯ»йӯҡдёҖжЁЈO.OгҖҚгҖҢеҸ°иӮЎдё–з•Ңеј·е•ҠгҖҚгҖҢе°Ҹж•ЈжҲ¶е№ҫ ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў зҫҺдјҠжҲ°дәӢз·Ҡејөжғ…еӢўзөӮйңІжӣҷе…үпјҢдјҠжң—зёҪзөұйҰ–еәҰй¬ҶеҸЈгҖҢйЎҳзөҗжқҹжҲ°зҲӯгҖҚпјҢзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®д№ҹжҡ—зӨәжҲ°дәӢеҸҜиғҪдёҚжңғжҢҒзәҢеӨӘд№…пјҢжҝҖеӢөеҸ°иӮЎд»ҠпјҲ1пјүж—ҘеҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёеӨ§жјІ1416.92й»һиҮі33,139.91й»һгҖӮ
вҖў ж°ёиӘ еңӢйҡӣжҠ•йЎ§еҲҶжһҗеё«жқҺеҝ иҲҲжҢҮеҮәпјҢжӯӨжіўеҸҚеҪҲйҷӨдәҶеҸ—жғ ж–јзҫҺиӮЎиө°еј·жҲ–ең°з·Јж”ҝжІ»йҷҚжә«пјҢй—ңйҚөеңЁж–јеүҚдёҖж—ҘиһҚиіҮеӨ§е№…жёӣиӮҘпјҢеёӮе ҙжө®йЎҚе·Іжңүж•Ҳжё…жҙ—гҖӮ
вҖў жқҺеҝ иҲҲиӘҚзӮәпјҢз•¶еүҚжғ…еӢўиҲҮеҺ»е№ҙжҳҺйЎҜдёҚеҗҢпјҢжҠҖиЎ“йқўдёӯжңҹи¶ЁеӢўд»ҚеҒҸеӨҡпјҢзҹӯз·ҡиӢҘеӣ еҲ©з©әжӢүеӣһпјҢеҸҚиҖҢжҳҜеҒҸеӨҡжҖқиҖғдёӢзҡ„еёғеұҖж©ҹжңғгҖӮ
вҖў ж“ҚдҪңзӯ–з•ҘдёҠпјҢе»әиӯ°жҠ•иіҮдәәдҝқз•ҷ3жҲҗзҸҫйҮ‘еӣ жҮүйңҮзӣӘпјҢз•ҷж„ҸеҖӢиӮЎеј·ејұеҲҶеҢ–пјҢеј·еӢўиӮЎеҸҜзәҢжҠұз”ҡиҮійҖўдҪҺеёғеұҖгҖӮ

news.Title: TSMC plans 3-nanometre chip production launch in Japan in 2028
news.Content: By Wen-Yee Lee TAIPEI, April 1 (Reuters) - TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, is expected to launch вҖҢequipment installation and mass production of 3-nanometre wafers ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў TSMC plans to launch equipment installation and mass production of 3-nanometre chips at its second factory in Japan in 2028.
вҖў The second Japanese fab will have a monthly production capacity of 15,000 12-inch wafers using advanced 3-nanometre process technology.
вҖў Total investment in TSMC's first and second fabs in Japan is expected to exceed $20 billion, with a combined monthly capacity of 100,000 wafers using less advanced technologies.
вҖў TSMC's first fab in Japan began volume production in late 2024.
вҖў Minority investors in TSMC's Japan unit include Sony Semiconductor Solutions Corporation, DENSO Corporation, and Toyota Motor Corporation.
news.Keywords: semiconductor manufacturing, advanced process technology, capital investment, global expansion, supply chain

news.Content: пјҲдёӯеӨ®зӨҫиЁҳиҖ…йҚҫжҰ®еі°еҸ°еҢ—2026е№ҙ04жңҲ1ж—Ҙйӣ»пјүзҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®иЎЁзӨәзҫҺи»ҚгҖҢеҫҲеҝ«гҖҚе°ҮзөҗжқҹеңЁдјҠжң—иЎҢеӢ•пјҢй җиЁҲ2еҲ°3йҖұе…§ж’ӨйӣўпјҢдјҠжң—зёҪзөұиЈҙжҫӨж–Ҝеҹәе®үд№ҹйҮӢеҮәе’Ңз·© ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў зҫҺеңӢзёҪзөұе·қжҷ®иЎЁзӨәзҫҺи»ҚгҖҢеҫҲеҝ«гҖҚе°ҮзөҗжқҹеңЁдјҠжң—иЎҢеӢ•пјҢй җиЁҲ2еҲ°3йҖұе…§ж’ӨйӣўпјҢдјҠжң—зёҪзөұиЈҙжҫӨж–Ҝеҹәе®үд№ҹйҮӢеҮәе’Ңз·©иЁҠжҒҜпјҢеёӮе ҙжЁӮи§Җжңҹеҫ…дёӯжқұиЎқзӘҒе°ҮзөҗжқҹпјҢзҫҺиӮЎеӨ§жјІпјҢжҝҖеӢөеҸ°иӮЎд»ҠеӨ©еј·еӢўеҸҚеҪҲеӨ§жјІйҖҫеҚғй»һпјҢзӣӨдёӯжҢҒзәҢеј·жјІи¶…йҒҺ1300й»һгҖӮ
вҖў еҠ ж¬ҠжҢҮж•ёеҲ°10жҷӮ45еҲҶзӮә33105.57й»һпјҢеӨ§жјІ1382.58й»һпјҢжјІе№…4.36%пјҢжҲҗдәӨеҖјзҙ„ж–°еҸ°е№Ј3593е„„е…ғгҖӮ
вҖў ж¬ҠзҺӢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зӣӨдёӯжңҖй«ҳ1845е…ғпјҢжјІ85е…ғпјҢжјІе№…4.83%пјҢеёӮеҖјжҡҙеўһж–°еҸ°е№Ј2.2е…Ҷе…ғгҖӮ
вҖў еҸ°ж–°еҸ°зҒЈICиЁӯиЁҲз ”з©¶еңҳйҡҠиЎЁзӨәпјҢAIжҮүз”ЁйңҖжұӮжҢҒзәҢзҶұзөЎпјҢжҸҗдҫӣеҸ°иӮЎдёӯй•·з·ҡдёҠж”»еӢ•иғҪгҖӮ

news.AI_Summary:
вҖў 3жңҲеҸ°иӮЎйЈҪеҸ—дёӯжқұжҲ°зҒ«иЎқж“ҠпјҢйҡЁе·қжҷ®гҖҢдәҢйҖұеҒңжҲ°иӘӘгҖҚеҲ©еӨҡеҮәйһҳпјҢеё¶ж—әеҸ°иӮЎиӯ·еңӢзҘһеұұеҸ°з©Қйӣ»еҸҠй«ҳеҗ«з©ҚйҮҸETFиӮЎеғ№иЈңиЎҖеҫ©з”ҰгҖӮ
вҖў е…¶дёӯпјҢж–°е…ү00904гҖҒе…ғеӨ§0050гҖҒеҜҢйӮҰ0052гҖҒеңӢжі°00922иҲҮеҮұеҹә009816е…ұ4жӘ”й«ҳеҗ«з©ҚйҮҸETFпјҢ3жңҲеҸ—зӣҠдәәж•ёж”ҖеҚҮиҮіжӯ·еҸІж–°й«ҳгҖӮ
вҖў зёҫж•Ҳж–№йқўпјҢж–°е…ү00904д»Ҡе№ҙдҫҶиҮі3/31зҙҜз©Қеҗ«жҒҜе ұй…¬зҺҮйҒ”20.11%пјҢи¶…и¶ҠеҸ°з©Қйӣ»зҡ„13.92%гҖӮ
вҖў еҚҠе°Һй«”иЎҢжҘӯжӯЈиҝҺдҫҶж–°дёҖијӘжјІеғ№йҖұжңҹпјҢзөҗж§ӢжҖ§зҡ„жҠ•иіҮж©ҹжңғжӯЈеңЁжө®зҸҫгҖӮ

news.Title: 3 Tech Stocks That Are Growing Revenue Faster Than Nvidia
news.Content: Nvidia has become the most notable stock success story in the current artificial intelligence (AI) boom. Last year, the company had reported several quarters of triple-digit revenue growth, a notable accomplishment for a company with a $4 trillion market cap. Even in the current quarter, revenue grew by an astounding 73% from a year ago. This feat is difficult to match even for much smaller companies ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Micron Technology reported $23.9 billion in revenue for fiscal Q2 2026, a 196% growth rate, driven by high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI applications.
вҖў Analysts predict 191% revenue growth for Micron in the current fiscal year, with its stock up 260% over the last year.
вҖў CoreWeave's Q4 revenue reached almost $1.6 billion, a 110% increase, driven by its AI-native cloud platform, with a backlog of $67 billion.
вҖў Nebius Group's Q4 revenue of $228 million saw a 547% increase, driven by its AI-focused cloud platform.
вҖў While Nebius Group has a strong financial position with $3.8 billion in cash and $4.1 billion in debt, its P/S ratio of 41 and recent stock surge may limit near-term upside.
news.Keywords: revenue growth, AI infrastructure, memory chips, cloud platform, valuation

вҖў еҸ°з©Қйӣ»д»Ҡж—Ҙй–ӢзӣӨеӨ§жјІ85е…ғпјҢиӮЎеғ№дҫҶеҲ°1845е…ғпјҢжјІе№…йҒ”4.83%пјҢжҲҗзӮәжҺЁеҚҮеӨ§зӣӨйЈҶжјІзҡ„жңҖдё»иҰҒеӢ•иғҪгҖӮ

news.Title: Apple's 50-year journey from garage to tech titan
news.Content: By Aditya Soni and Akash Sriram April 1 (Reuters) - In early 1976 in California, Steve Wozniak had just completed the design of a computer circuit board he intended to share with fellow hobbyists at a prominent local club. His friend вҖҢSteve Jobs also saw a business opportunity to manufacture and sell the boards, and thus Apple ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Apple is facing pressure to demonstrate its continued relevance in the age of artificial intelligence, with rivals Alphabet and Microsoft investing heavily.
вҖў Apple's stock has been the second-worst performer among the 'Magnificent Seven' since the launch of ChatGPT in November 2022.
вҖў Analysts and investors suggest Apple was unprepared for the consumer adoption of AI, citing delays in features like a revamped Siri.
вҖў Despite AI challenges, strong demand for the iPhone 17 series and the MacBook Neo contributed to Apple's recent earnings.
вҖў Apple is projected to post sales of $465 billion in its ongoing fiscal year ending in September.
news.Keywords: AI strategy, market competition, product demand, revenue projections, stock performance

news.AI_Summary:
вҖў дјҠжң—иЎЁзӨәж“Ғжңүеҝ…иҰҒж„Ҹеҝ—зөҗжқҹжҲ°зҲӯпјҢе°ӢжұӮдҝқиӯүиЎқзӘҒдёҚйҮҚжј”пјҢж¶ҲжҒҜжҝҖеӢөзҫҺиӮЎеҸҚеҪҲпјҢйҒ“з“Ҡе·ҘжҘӯжҢҮж•ёйЈҶжјІйҖҫ1100й»һпјҢиІ»еҹҺеҚҠе°Һй«”жҢҮж•ёеӢҒжҸҡ6.24%пјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»ADRеј·еҪҲ6.78%гҖӮ
вҖў еҸ°жҢҮжңҹеӨңзӣӨеӨ§жјІ1130й»һж”¶33084й»һпјҢжі•дәәй җжңҹеҸ°иӮЎд»ҠеӨ©жңүж©ҹжңғеҸҚеҪҲпјҢж”¶еҫ©еӯЈз·ҡзҙ„32182й»һеҸҜжңҹпјҢжҢ‘жҲ°33000й»һй—ңеҚЎгҖӮ
вҖў еёӮе ҙи§Ји®ҖзҫҺдјҠжҲ°дәӢеҸҜжңӣзөҗжқҹпјҢзҫҺиӮЎ4еӨ§жҢҮж•ёеј·еҪҲгҖӮ
вҖў дёүеӨ§жі•дәә3жңҲ31ж—ҘеҗҲиЁҲиіЈи¶…еҸ°иӮЎ986.64е„„е…ғпјҢеӨ–иіҮеҸҠйҷёиіҮиіЈи¶…807.45е„„е…ғпјҢеүөеҸІдёҠеӨ–иіҮжңҖеӨ§е–®жңҲиіЈи¶…йҮ‘йЎҚгҖӮ

news.Title: AI Data Centers Are Choking on Memory: Is Micron a Buy After Its Monster Run?
news.Content: Shares of Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) have soared over the past year due to a shortage of high-performance memory for data centers. The company could see strong financial performance over the next few years, as demand remains well above supply. The key question investors need to answer before buying Micron today is whether ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Micron's revenue tripled year-over-year to nearly $24 billion in the recent quarter, with earnings per share rising to $12.07 from $1.41.
вҖў AI computing systems require advanced memory, and the industry is currently unable to meet demand, with Micron fulfilling only 50-67% of customer requests.
вҖў The memory shortage is expected to persist through the end of 2026 and into next year, supporting higher earnings.
вҖў Demand for memory is also growing in consumer devices and emerging applications like humanoid robots.
вҖў Analysts project earnings per share to climb to $58 in fiscal 2026 and $98 in fiscal 2027, before potentially declining to $77 in fiscal 2028, indicating potential cyclicality.
news.Keywords: memory shortage, AI demand, revenue growth, gross margin, cyclical risk

news.Title: Duos Technologies Group Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
news.Content: Key Points Strategic pivot: New CEO Doug Recker said Duos will "completely divest the rail division" within ~60 days and is now fully dedicated to the data center market via its Duos Edge and Duos Technology Solutions units to free resources and cut SG&A. Commercial scale-up: After $45M and $65M capital raises, Duos says it has procured/manufactured 15 edge data centers and will deploy ~2,300 GPUs, including a GPU-as-a-Service contract for 2,304 NVIDIA GPUs expected to generate about $176M over 36 months and a 4.8 MW high-power colocation deal for a hyperscaler. Financial momentum: 2025 revenue rose to roughly $27M ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Duos Technologies Group (DUOT) is strategically pivoting to the data center market, divesting its rail division.
вҖў The company procured/manufactured 15 edge data centers, deploying ~2,300 GPUs, including a $176M GPU-as-a-Service contract with Nvidia.
вҖў 2025 revenue rose ~270% YoY to $27M with a 29% gross margin.
вҖў Duos guided 2026 revenue of $50вҖ“$55M and has a pipeline of over $150 million for its new Technology Solutions division.
news.Keywords: data center, GPU deployment, revenue growth, strategic pivot, AI infrastructure

news.Title: NvidiaвҖҷs stock split history: Everything you need to know
news.Content: Companies conduct stock splits to allow investors to purchase their shares at a more affordable price. Tech giant and Magnificent 7 leader Nvidia is no exception, having split its stock several times in the more than 25 years itвҖҷs been a publicly traded company. If demand for the AI processing leader's shares continues to increase, pushing its stock price up further in the years to come, Nvidia may have to conduct more stock splits. HereвҖҷs a history of NvidiaвҖҷs stock splits, and a look at how high its share price would be today had it not multiplied ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia has conducted six stock splits in its history, with the most recent being a 10-for-1 split in June 2024.
вҖў Stock splits increase the number of shares available for trading and make the stock more affordable for individual investors.
вҖў Nvidia's stock price would be significantly higher today without these splits.
вҖў Splits can also affect dividends per share and provide flexibility for employee stock options and buybacks.
news.Keywords: stock split, share price, affordability, investor access, corporate actions

news.Title: Mega-cap dividend stock targets $9 trillion valuation
news.Content: Meta Platforms recently disclosed a new stock option plan for its top executives that won't pay out unless the company's market cap skyrockets from roughly $1.36 trillion to $9 trillion by 2031 . That would make Meta worth more than twice Nvidia 's value , currently the world's most valuable company at around $4.1 trillion. For dividend stock investors watching Meta, the new plan provides a signal about where the company is betting its future and how confident it is getting there. Meta's executive compensation plan: the $9 ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Meta Platforms (META) has a new stock option plan for executives tied to a market cap target of $9 trillion by 2031.
вҖў This plan signals Meta's confidence in massive future success, potentially making it worth more than twice Nvidia's current value.
вҖў Meta initiated its first-ever dividend in early 2024 and estimates 2026 dividend per share at $2.24, with a 12.6% CAGR through 2030.
вҖў Free cash flow is projected to drop 75.1% in 2026 due to AI infrastructure investments but is expected to more than double by 2028.
news.Keywords: market cap, executive compensation, dividend, free cash flow, AI infrastructure

news.Content: News hit today that Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) intends to invest approximately $2 billion in Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) . Nvidia's forefront position in AI processors and software development platforms has made the company the single most influential player in the artificial-intelligence space. The distinction lends added importance to investment moves ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia (NVDA) plans to invest approximately $2 billion in Marvell Technology (MRVL), a semiconductor design company specializing in data-center infrastructure.
вҖў Marvell is a leader in ASICs, a technological alternative to Nvidia's GPUs in the AI processing market.
вҖў Nvidia's investment is a bullish indicator, with Marvell's share price surging roughly 13% on the news.
вҖў The companies are also deepening their partnership to develop silicon photonics and other AI-centric telecommunications infrastructure hardware.
news.Keywords: investment, AI hardware, semiconductor, partnership, silicon photonics

news.Title: American Exchange Is Set to Acquire Allbirds for $39 Million. Here's What Investors Need to Know.
news.Content: Allbirds' (Nasdaq: BIRD) fall from grace is now complete. The once-promising footwear maker said after hours Monday that it would sell itself to American Exchange, the owner of Aerosoles, for just $39 million, or roughly ten times less than what it raised in its ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Allbirds will be acquired by American Exchange for $39 million, a fraction of its IPO valuation.
вҖў The footwear maker experienced significant errors, including product line expansion, durability issues, and aggressive retail expansion.
вҖў Allbirds' revenue has steadily eroded since peaking in 2022, with no quarters of positive revenue growth in over three years.
вҖў The company's downfall serves as a lesson on managing growth, brand dilution, and consumer market fickleness.
news.Keywords: acquisition, footwear, revenue decline, brand dilution, consumer market

news.Title: NvidiaвҖҷs $2 billion bet reveals its next big target
news.Content: Nvidia ( NVDA ) started as a video game chipmaker, but became so much more thanks to smart investments. Today, along with GPUs, it makes CPUs, networking equipment, and AI inference accelerators. According to the companyвҖҷs 10-K form , in fiscal year 2026, its networking revenue totaled $31.4 billion. That was a pretty big jump from $13 billion in 2025. The form states: вҖңRevenue from Data Center networking grew 142%, driven by the introduction and continued ramp of NVLink compute fabric for GB200 and GB300 systems and the growth of ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia invested $2 billion in Lumentum Holdings (LITE) and Coherent Corp. (COHR), both networking companies focused on silicon photonics.
вҖў Nvidia's networking revenue totaled $31.4 billion in fiscal year 2026, a significant jump from $13 billion in 2025.
вҖў Nvidia is strengthening its position in silicon photonics through partnerships and investments to compete with Broadcom.
вҖў Nvidia also partnered with Marvell Technology (MRVL), investing $2 billion and integrating Marvell's custom AI accelerators into Nvidia's NVLink Fusion platform.
news.Keywords: silicon photonics, networking, investment, strategic partnership, AI infrastructure

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: OpenAI valued at $852 billion in latest funding round ahead of IPO
news.Content: OpenAI ( OPAI.PVT ) on Tuesday announced it closed its latest funding round, raising $122 billion in commitments at a valuation of $852 billion. ThatвҖҷs more than the $110 billion the company said it raised in February when it was valued at $730 billion. In a statement , OpenAI said it is now generating $2 billion in revenue per month, up from $1 billion per quarter in 2024, ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў OpenAI closed a funding round with $122 billion in commitments at an $852 billion valuation, up from $730 billion in February.
вҖў OpenAI is generating $2 billion in revenue per month, growing four times faster than Google parent Alphabet and Meta.
вҖў The funding round was led by Amazon ($50B), Nvidia ($30B), and SoftBank ($30B), with participation from Microsoft.
вҖў The capital will fund OpenAI's data-center buildout, estimated at $600 billion by 2030.
news.Keywords: funding round, valuation, revenue growth, data center, AI infrastructure

news.Title: Ignore the Panic and Buy the Dip in Micron Stock, Says Bank of America
news.Content: Micron Technology (MU) has found itself caught in a familiar market pattern вҖ” a knee-jerk reaction to a change in the way we think about a particular type of technology. In this case, the recent introduction of TurboQuant from Alphabet (GOOGL) вҖ” a program that promises to cut memory usage in half вҖ” is presenting AI efficiency fears. While this may initially be seen as a negative for memory and storage-related companies as a whole, if ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Bank of America analysts suggest AI capital expenditures are driving memory usage, countering fears of efficiency gains from new technologies like Alphabet's TurboQuant.
вҖў Micron's stock has fallen 28% from its 52-week high and 15% in the last five days, presenting a potential buying opportunity.
вҖў Micron reported exceptional Q2 fiscal 2026 results with revenue of $23.86 billion (up from $8.05 billion YoY) and GAAP EPS of $12.07 (up from $1.41 YoY).
вҖў Gross margins exceeded 74% in the most recent quarter, and the data-center segment revenue more than doubled to almost $5.7 billion.
вҖў Memory is now considered a strategic, structural asset in AI infrastructure, with no signs of weakening demand due to efficiency gains.
news.Keywords: memory market, AI capex, stock valuation, gross margin, structural demand

news.Title: 1 AI Software Stock That Can Outperform Palantir Over the Next Year
news.Content: Few software stocks have matched Palantir 's (NASDAQ: PLTR) performance over the last few years. While Palantir's stock has been caught up in the broader sell-off among SaaS stocks , the company looks fairly resilient. news.AI_Summary:

вҖў Palantir's revenue grew 70% year-over-year in Q4, with an adjusted operating margin of 57%.
вҖў The company's Maven system is designated as a program of record by the DoD, potentially worth billions.

news.Title: Nvidia investors just had a surprising first quarter

вҖў Analysts note continued uncertainty around the growth trajectory for Nvidia's data center business in 2027.
news.Keywords: stock performance, AI inference, valuation concerns, data center growth, market sentiment

news.Title: North Korea-linked hack hits largely invisible software that powers online services
news.Content: By AJ Vicens March 31 (Reuters) - Hackers linked to North Korea breached behind-the-scenes software that runs many common online functions in an effort to steal login information that could вҖҢenable further cyber operations, Google said on Tuesday. The hackers ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Hackers linked to North Korea breached Axios, a behind-the-scenes software used by many online functions, to steal login information.
вҖў The breach occurred via a malicious software update, representing a supply chain attack.
вҖў Google attributed the attack to UNC1069, a group known for targeting cryptocurrency and financial industries.
вҖў The malicious software could infect macOS, Windows, and Linux systems, potentially reaching millions of environments.
news.Keywords: cybersecurity, supply chain attack, data breach, North Korea, malware

news.Title: Why Palantir's New Program of Record With the Pentagon Could Be a Game Changer
news.Content: In early March, Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve Feinberg designated the Maven Smart System (MSS) from Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) as a formal program of record . This move will transition the platform from niche, experimental use cases into a standardized, long-term fixture in U.S. military operations. For Palantir, this move by the Pentagon ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Palantir Technologies' (PLTR) Maven Smart System (MSS) has been designated a formal program of record by the Pentagon.
вҖў This move standardizes MSS for U.S. military operations, securing multiyear funding for Palantir.
вҖў The MSS program has scaled rapidly, with a five-year contract for the U.S. Army worth up to $1.3 billion.
вҖў This endorsement validates Palantir's ontology platform and is expected to supercharge its government segment as AI adoption accelerates.
news.Keywords: defense, AI, program of record, government contract, revenue visibility

news.Title: The AI Supercycle Has a New Bottleneck. 1 Growth Stock Is Positioned to Solve It.
news.Content: When most investors think of the artificial intelligence ( AI ) market, they often focus on leading chipmakers like Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) or hyperscalers like Microsoft . However, those AI leaders can't keep expanding without companies like Lumentum (NASDAQ: LITE) providing the optical equipment ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Lumentum (LITE) stock surged nearly 1,000% in the past 12 months as hyperscalers increased networking spending for AI applications.
вҖў Lumentum provides optical networking equipment that addresses bottlenecks in data transfer speeds for AI GPUs.
вҖў Nvidia invested $2 billion in Lumentum and formed a strategic partnership in early March.
вҖў Analysts expect Lumentum's revenue to surge at a 58% CAGR from 2025 to 2028, reaching $6.4 billion.

news.Title: Wall Street resets Amazon stock price targets on AWS AI trends
news.Content: Amazon stock is down about 11% year-to-date, but Wall Street is starting to lean more bullish again. The shift comes as AWS growth reaccelerates, driven by strong demand for AI workloads. At the same time, AmazonвҖҷs advertising business continues to scale into a larger profit driver, giving the company a stronger long-term earnings base. ThatвҖҷs what makes the stock interesting right now. Amazon is pulling its business in two directions at once. Demand across cloud and AI is clearly strengthening, but the company is also preparing to spend heavily to support that growth. Amazon valuation snapshot Market cap: $2.14 trillion ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Amazon stock is down 11% year-to-date, but Wall Street is becoming more bullish due to reaccelerating AWS growth driven by AI workloads.
вҖў Citi and JPMorgan raised Amazon price targets to $285 and $280 respectively, expecting AWS to grow 28-37% in 2026-2027.
вҖў AWS revenue rose 24% YoY in Q4 2025 to $35.6 billion, with operating margin at 35.0%.
вҖў Amazon plans to spend roughly $200 billion in 2026 capital expenditures on AI infrastructure, impacting free cash flow.
news.Keywords: AWS growth, AI workloads, capital expenditure, price target, cloud computing

news.Title: This Magnificent Software Stock Is Down 35%. Buy It Before It Sets a New All-Time High.
news.Content: The weakness since November makes enough superficial sense. That's when the world began rethinking the actual value of artificial intelligence (AI), as well as most AI stocks' steep valuations. Now down 35% from its late-October peak , however, the sellers have arguably overshot their target when it comes to Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) . That means opportunity for you. The stage was set ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Microsoft (MSFT) shares are down 35% from their late-October peak, potentially presenting a buying opportunity.
вҖў The pullback is attributed to concerns about Microsoft's AI business, including Copilot's low market share (3%) and slowing Azure revenue growth.
вҖў Microsoft intends to spend $120 billion on AI infrastructure this fiscal year, but investors question the return on investment.
вҖў Despite the pullback, Microsoft's core businesses (Windows, Office) remain strong, and AI capacity is expected to translate into revenue over time.
news.Keywords: stock pullback, AI business, Copilot, Azure growth, capital expenditure

news.Title: Down 80%, Is Duolingo Stock a Buy Now?
news.Content: Shares of Duolingo (NASDAQ: DUOL) have fallen more than 80% from their height, a sharp reversal for one of the market's former high-growth favorites. After peaking above $500, the stock now trades closer to $100. That kind of drop naturally raises a key question: Is this a buying opportunity or a warning sign? To answer that, you need to understand why the stock fell and what the company is doing now to improve ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Duolingo's (DUOL) stock fell over 80% despite solid 2025 results (revenue up 39%, net profit tripled).
вҖў The decline is attributed to slowing user growth (expected to slow to 20% in 2026) due to increased monetization efforts.
вҖў Duolingo is prioritizing user growth in 2026, even if it sacrifices near-term profitability, targeting 100 million daily users by 2028.
вҖў The success of this strategy depends on improving user experience and engagement, with AI tools potentially enhancing personalization.
news.Keywords: user growth, monetization, AI tools, strategic reset, stock decline

news.Title: Individual Investors Could Get a Rare Shot at Buying Into SpaceX From Day One
news.Content: Love him or hate him, we all can admit that Elon Musk has a knack for generating excitement. His next attempt at it may come with SpaceX's upcoming initial public offering ( IPO ), planned for June of this year. In what could be the largest IPO in history, SpaceX may finally enter the public markets, and there may be a surprise for retail investors along the way. Here's the skinny on SpaceX's reported plans, ...

вҖў SpaceX is planning its IPO in June, potentially raising $40-$80 billion, with an ambitious plan for space-based AI data centers.

news.Title: Goldman Sachs has a message on Nvidia stock for investors
news.Content: The Goldman Sachs trading desk has circulated a note flagging what it describes as a significant disconnect in Nvidia stock. The core observation is that NVDA is now trading at a forward price-to-earnings ratio below the S&P 500 for the first time in more than a decade, even as the company continues to deliver some of the strongest earnings growth in the index. According to the Goldman Sachs trading desk note, Nvidia is trading at approximately 19.7 times forward earnings. The S&P ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Goldman Sachs notes a significant disconnect: Nvidia (NVDA) trades at a forward P/E below the S&P 500 for the first time in over a decade.
вҖў Nvidia's Q4 revenue was $68.1 billion (up 73% YoY), with Data Center revenue at $62.3 billion (up 75% YoY).
вҖў The valuation compression reflects broader repricing of high-growth tech stocks due to macro forces like interest rates and geopolitical tensions.
вҖў Nvidia sees at least $1 trillion in purchase orders for Blackwell and Vera Rubin chips through 2027, and guided for $78 billion in Q1 revenue.
news.Keywords: valuation disconnect, P/E ratio, revenue growth, macro factors, AI infrastructure

news.Title: Elon Musk reveals 'plus side' of quantum computing breaking Bitcoin
news.Content: The worldвҖҷs wealthiest person, Elon Musk , is known for his deep interest in Bitcoin (BTC) and rarely holds back from sharing his views whenever the topic comes up. So when Google issued a new warning about the potential threat quantum computing poses to Bitcoin, he quickly responded with ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Google warns that quantum computers could crack Bitcoin cryptography by 2029, potentially enabling theft of crypto assets.
вҖў A quantum system could crack a real-time Bitcoin transaction in about nine minutes, requiring fewer qubits than previously estimated.
вҖў Google recommends transitioning to post-quantum cryptography (PQC) to address these threats.
вҖў Elon Musk commented that a 'plus side' of quantum computing is that forgotten wallet passwords could become accessible in the future.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, Bitcoin, cryptography, cybersecurity, crypto assets

news.Title: Trump Tax LawвҖҷs Affordable Housing Boost Hits Snag
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- The US governmentвҖҷs largest affordable housing program was supposed to be supercharged by President Donald TrumpвҖҷs signature tax law. So far, that jolt hasnвҖҷt arrived. ThatвҖҷs because, investors and developers say, their efforts to take advantage of the dramatic expansion of the Low-Income Housing Tax Credit have bumped up against some unintended consequences. In particular, the law increased the potential size of any given affordable housing development deal eligible for the credit вҖ“ but that, in turn, means few banks are able to finance them under the complex rules of the program. вҖңThe market has slowed pretty ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The US government's affordable housing program faces challenges due to unintended consequences of a tax law.
вҖў Banks face limitations on financing large affordable housing deals due to caps on 'public welfare investments'.
вҖў Proposed legislation aims to raise these caps, but its passage is uncertain.
вҖў The lack of financing is stalling the development of new affordable housing units.
news.Keywords: Affordable Housing, Tax Credits, Financial Regulations, Housing Development, Investment Caps

news.Title: Buyback Watch: KLA, Flutter, and Grab Move Fast as Their Stocks Swing
news.Content:
Key Points

KLA has surged due to chip shortages, and the company just increased its buyback capacity in a big way.

As prediction market fears hit FLUT, analysts are indicating that a huge recovery may be ahead.

Regulatory issues are rattling GRAB, and the company now clearly sees value in its stock.

Interested in KLA Corporation? Here are five stocks we like better.

Despite putting up wildly different performances lately, big names across semiconductors, entertainment, and e-commerce are indicating significant confidence in their outlooks through recent buyback announcements. This includes two beaten-down stocks that are looking to spend hundreds of millions on repurchases in relatively short order. With this, these companies likely see opportunity in their shares near current levels.

Semi Equipment Giant KLA Ups Buyback Capacity to $11 Billion

KLA (NASDAQ: KLAC) has been one of the marketвҖҷs biggest large-cap winners recently, with shares up more than 100% over the past 52 weeks. This comes as the firm is one of the worldвҖҷs top providers of semiconductor manufacturing equipment.

вҶ’ Down 25%, Chinese Giant PDD Could Be a Strong Long-Term Value

With the supply of leading-edge wafers and high-bandwidth memory constrained, equipment providers like KLA are likely to see robust demand going forward. After posting 7% year-over-year (YOY) growth last quarter, KLAвҖҷs guidance implies an acceleration to 9% growth next quarter. Currently, Wall Street forecasts see KLAвҖҷs revenue growth accelerating in each of the next five quarters.

Further supporting the companyвҖҷs outlook is the $7 billion share repurchase plan KLAC recently announced. This comes as shares are down more than 10% from their 52-week high. It is possible that KLA believes the market reacted illogically to its last earnings report, as shares tanked 15% the next day. Thus, the company may see an opportunity in its share price, even as the stock has surged.

вҶ’ Coursera's Options Anomaly: A Big Bet on What's Next?

This authorization adds to the companyвҖҷs unused $3.94 billion in buyback capacity, giving it a total capacity of just under $11 billion. This is equal to a sizable 5.8% of the firmвҖҷs approximately $190 billion market capitalization, giving KLA ample ability to repurchase shares.

Down +50%, FLUT Announces 10-Week Buyback Plan

On the other side of the equation, shares of Flutter Entertainment (NYSE: FLUT) have taken a huge hit over the past 52-weeks, down over 55%. Flutter operates FanDuel. Depending on the metric used, FanDuel has the first or second-highest market share of the U.S. online sports betting market. DraftKings (NASDAQ: DKNG) is the only competitor that rivals Flutter in market share. However, many view the rise of prediction markets in 2025 as a significant threat to FlutterвҖҷs traditional online sports betting business.

Story Continues

вҶ’ Russell 2000 Stocks: Too Early or Finally Interesting?

In its last earnings report, the company said that it did not believe prediction markets were having a meaningful impact on its business. However, the company also said that handle growth, or betting volume growth, was moderating. This leads to concerns that prediction markets are causing bettors to look to other platforms. Ultimately, the company missed estimates on sales and adjusted earnings per share by a wide margin, and the stock fell almost 14% after the report.

Still, Flutter remains confident in its outlook, and the firm is ramping up its own prediction markets offering.

Demonstrating this confidence is the firmвҖҷs latest $250 million share repurchase arrangement. This arrangement is equal to a moderate 1.4% of the firmвҖҷs approximately $17.5 billion market capitalization. However, the company plans to execute the program over just 10 weeks. This suggests that Flutter wants to buy back its stock at a relatively quick pace to take advantage of recent prices.

GRAB Sees вҖңDislocationвҖқ in Shares, Announces $400 Buyback

Grab (NASDAQ: GRAB) is a dominant ride-hailing and food delivery platform in Southeast Asia. Within the region, the firmвҖҷs share of the food delivery market rose to 55% in 2025, up from 53.8% in 2024. Despite this, Grab is down over 20% during the past 52 weeks, and down more than 40% from its 52-week high.

Regulatory pressures have been a headwind for Grab as of late. For example, media outlets have reported that Indonesia, one of GrabвҖҷs largest markets, could take actions that significantly hurt GrabвҖҷs business.

This includes cutting the maximum commission cap that Grab can charge on rides from 20% to 10%. Grab notes that "if adopted, any such changes would increase our costs, reduce our margins, and diminish our operational flexibility."

Despite this, GrabвҖҷs guidance points to a strong year ahead, with the firm projecting 20% to 22% revenue growth in 2026. Meanwhile, it sees adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization rising by a range of 40% to 44%.

Grab also plans to deploy $400 million worth of share buybacks over the next four months. This would represent significant buyback spending over a short period of time, equal to around 2.7% of GrabвҖҷs approximately $14.6 billion market capitalization. Within this, $250 million will go toward an accelerated repurchase program, indicating that Grab feels a need to repurchase stock near current levels.

In its buyback announcement, Grab said, вҖңWe view the current share price dislocation as a clear opportunity to enhance shareholder value."

Could Regulators Help Turn FLUTвҖҷs Fortunes?

KLAC, FLUT, and GRAB are all moving to instill confidence in shareholders through these buyback announcements. As the Senate has introduced a bill to ban sports betting on prediction markets, Flutter is a particularly interesting stock going forward. The MarketBeat consensus price target on FLUT sits near $227, implying over 100% upside. However, targets updated after the companyвҖҷs last earnings report are significantly lower, near $183. Still, this figure implies over 80% upside in the stock.

The article " Buyback Watch: KLA, Flutter, and Grab Move Fast as Their Stocks Swing " was originally published by MarketBeat.

news.Title: OpenAI Just Pulled the Plug on Sora. Is This AI Stock the Biggest Winner?
news.Content: When the public first got a glimpse of Sora, OpenAI's text-to-video generative AI model, some saw the disruptive potential immediately. Perry told The Hollywood Reporter back in February 2024, "All of that is currently and indefinitely on hold because of Sora and what I'm seeing. I had gotten word over the last year or so that this was coming, but I had no idea until I saw recently the demonstrations of what ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: Unlocking Intrinsic Value: Analysis of Meta Platforms Inc
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . In this article, we will take a look into Meta Platforms Inc's ( NASDAQ:META ) DCF analysis, a reliable and data-driven approach to estimating its intrinsic value. Instead of using future free cash flow as in the traditional DCF model, the GuruFocus DCF calculator uses EPS without NRI as the default for the DCF model based on research that shows that historically stock prices have been more correlated with earnings than free cash ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Meta Platforms Inc.'s intrinsic value, calculated by GuruFocus's DCF model, is estimated at $730.56, suggesting it is modestly undervalued at its current trading price of $536.38.
вҖў The DCF model uses EPS without NRI and a two-stage growth approach, with a 10-year growth stage at 22.70% and a terminal stage at 4%.
вҖў A traditional DCF model based on free cash flow estimates the intrinsic value at $560.10, indicating fair valuation with a 4.23% margin of safety.
вҖў The model's accuracy depends on various assumptions and projections about future earnings potential and growth rates.
news.Keywords: DCF Analysis, Intrinsic Value, Valuation Model, Margin of Safety, Financial Projections

news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Tech megacaps entered a correction, oil prices broke out, big-money funds retreated and small-lot investors showed waning conviction in buying the dip. Technology stocks were hit particularly hard, owing to a double-whammy of geopolitical angst and worries over ...

вҖў The S&P 500 is on track for its worst month and quarter since 2022, with tech megacaps hit hard by geopolitical angst and AI disruption worries.
вҖў Microsoft (MSFT) has fallen 26% this year amid uncertainty over capital spending and competition from AI startups.
вҖў The Nasdaq 100 is in correction territory, trading at 21 times projected 12-month profits, a narrow gap with the S&P 500.

news.Keywords: Market Correction, Tech Sector Decline, Geopolitical Impact, Energy Stocks, AI Disruption

news.Title: If This Wall Street Analyst Is Correct, This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Data Center Stock Will Join Nvidia, Apple, and Meta in the $1 Trillion Club
news.Content: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) burst onto the scene last year as a big player in the artificial intelligence space, but its meteoric rise was short-lived. The stock has nearly been cut in half over the past six months, as investors question whether all of Oracle's deals will come to fruition, and whether the debt needed to fund its artificial intelligence (AI) data center build-out will ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Oracle's stock has fallen nearly 50% in six months amid questions about its AI data center build-out and debt funding.
вҖў The company reported strong AI demand and remaining performance obligations (RPOs) of $553 billion in Q3 fiscal 2026.
вҖў Analysts, like Guggenheim's John DiFucci, have bullish price targets suggesting significant upside potential for Oracle stock.
вҖў OpenAI's successful $120 billion private financing round adds confidence to its infrastructure commitments.
news.Keywords: AI Data Centers, Cloud Infrastructure, Stock Performance, Analyst Price Target, Financing Round

news.Title: This Monopoly Stock Powers Every AI Chip on the Planet, and It's Down Over 14% This Month
news.Content: It's rare to find a company that has a true monopoly on its industry. It's even rarer when that company has no meaningful competition in any form ready to break into the market and claim some of it. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue В» And it's rarer still when that company isn't ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) is the sole provider of EUV lithography machines essential for producing advanced semiconductors used in AI.
вҖў ASML's machines are critical for chip manufacturers producing 7nm or smaller semiconductors.
вҖў The company is innovating its EUV technology, planning to boost machine power by 50% by the end of the decade.
вҖў ASML's net bookings more than doubled in Q4 2025, indicating high and accelerating demand for its machines.
news.Keywords: EUV Lithography, Semiconductor Manufacturing, AI Chips, Market Monopoly, Demand Growth

news.Title: 1 Magnificent Growth Stock Down 32% That Smart Money Is Quietly Accumulating
news.Content: Increased market turmoil is hitting the Magnificent Seven, with Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) taking the hardest hit out of all of them. It has fallen 33.5% from its 52-week high. A general sell-off in artificial intelligence (AI) stocks impacted the company amid massive capital expenditures (capex) spending. Investors also harbor doubts about its partnership with OpenAI. However, amid these conditions, ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Microsoft (MSFT) stock has fallen 33.5% from its 52-week high, impacted by AI stock sell-offs and massive capital expenditures.
вҖў Despite concerns about OpenAI's financial stability, Microsoft has a $344 billion backlog excluding OpenAI's commitments.
вҖў Institutional investors, including Vanguard and BlackRock, increased their Microsoft holdings in Q4 2025.
вҖў The stock's pullback is seen as a contrarian buy opportunity, with institutions showing confidence in its long-term prospects.
news.Keywords: Contrarian Buy, AI Capex, Institutional Holdings, Stock Pullback, Cloud Computing

news.Title: Apple watch rival Whoop is raising $575 million from investors including Cristiano Ronaldo, LeBron James
news.Content: Whoop ( WHOO.PVT ) doesn't appear to be running toward an IPO, but it got another healthy injection of capital. The wearable device company вҖ” which dubs itself a "human performance company" вҖ” said Tuesday it raised $575 million in Series G funding at a $10.1 billion valuation. Rival Oura Health, the maker of the Oura Ring, completed a $140 million Series ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Wearable device company Whoop raised $575 million in Series G funding at a $10.1 billion valuation, led by Collaborative Fund.
вҖў Prominent athletes like Cristiano Ronaldo and LeBron James participated in the funding round.
вҖў Whoop has 2.5 million members and grew bookings by 103% year over year, with a bookings run rate of $1.1 billion.
вҖў The company's latest innovations include FDA clearance for medical-grade ECG and blood pressure monitoring on its Whoop MG model.
news.Keywords: Wearable Technology, Funding Round, Health Monitoring, AI Coach, Subscription Model

news.Title: TrumpвҖҷs Tax Cut Delivers at Least $65 Billion Windfall to Corporations
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Some of the countryвҖҷs wealthiest corporations are calculating they owe far less to the Internal Revenue Service as a result of President Donald TrumpвҖҷs overhauled tax code, underscoring how a law billed as a middleвҖ‘class cut also turned out to be a big win for Corporate America. Nearly a dozen of the 50 biggest US-listed companies attributed a drop in federal cash income taxes last year as a direct result of TrumpвҖҷs $3.4 trillion sweeping tax law, according to a Bloomberg analysis of regulatory filings. In all, annual corporate tax revenues dropped by $65 billion following the lawвҖҷs ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Amazon.com Inc. saw a significant drop in federal cash income taxes in 2025, owing $2.8 billion down from over $7 billion in prior years.
вҖў The company attributes this decrease to Trump's tax law, which it expects to continue reducing cash taxes in 2026.
вҖў The tax law provided benefits across various sectors, including Big Tech, retailers, and manufacturers, through deductions and special preferences.
вҖў Corporate tax revenues dropped by $65 billion annually following the law's passage.
news.Keywords: Tax Law Impact, Corporate Taxes, Tax Reduction, Regulatory Filings, Fiscal Policy

news.Title: This Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Is Beating the S&P 500, the Nasdaq-100, and the Dow Jones in 2026
news.Content: The U.S. stock market is off to a difficult start to 2026. Ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have sent the price of oil soaring, which is creating economic instability and clouding the earnings picture for corporate America. As a result, the S&P 500 , the Nasdaq-100 , and the Dow Jones Industrial Average have each suffered year-to-date declines of more than 5% so far. However, another index is bucking the losses to trade flat for the year. The Russell 2000 is home to approximately 2,000 ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The Vanguard Russell 2000 ETF (VTWO) is trading flat for the year, outperforming the S&P 500, Nasdaq-100, and Dow Jones.
вҖў Small-cap stocks in the Russell 2000 are more insulated from global conflicts due to their domestic focus.
вҖў The ETF's diversification and low weighting of top holdings (5.6%) contribute to steadier returns compared to concentrated indexes.
вҖў Companies like Bloom Energy and Credo Technology within the ETF have shown significant stock price appreciation due to strong demand in their respective sectors.
news.Keywords: Small-Cap Stocks, ETF Performance, Diversification, Market Insulation, Sector Demand

вҖў The ETF's diversification and low weighting of top holdings (5.6%) contribute to steadier returns compared to concentrated indexes.
вҖў Companies like Bloom Energy and Credo Technology within the ETF have shown significant stock price appreciation due to strong demand in their respective sectors.
news.Keywords: Small-Cap Stocks, ETF Performance, Diversification, Market Insulation, Sector Demand

news.Title: This Is the Cheapest "Magnificent Seven" Stock Right Now. Is it a Value Play or Value Trap?
news.Content: The once seemingly invincible "Magnificent Seven" stocks have not fared well this year. The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF has fallen by more than 9%, underperforming the broader market. Investors may see this as a buying opportunity, given that most still see immense potential with artificial intelligence (AI). But one should never buy a stock or an exchange-traded fund (ETF) simply because it is down. Valuation is also important. This is the cheapest Magnificent Seven stock right now. Is it a value play or a value trap? Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Meta Platforms (META) has the lowest valuation among the Magnificent Seven, trading at less than 20 times forward earnings.
вҖў Meta projects significant capex in 2026 ($115-$135 billion) to support AI infrastructure, including third-party cloud providers and data centers.
вҖў Investors are concerned about Zuckerberg's tendency to make large, potentially undisciplined bets on new technologies.
вҖў Meta's ad revenue grew 24% YoY in 2025, with AI expected to enhance ad targeting and engagement.
news.Keywords: Magnificent Seven, Valuation Analysis, AI Capex, Advertising Revenue, Investment Discipline

news.Title: Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights Adobe's and Microsoft
news.Content: For Immediate Release Chicago, IL – March 31, 2026 – Today, Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights Adobe’s ADBE and Microsoft MSFT. 2 Great Tech Stocks to Buy Now, Down -65% and -35% Artificial intelligence expansion and innovations rocked software stocks in the first quarter of 2026. Wall Street is worried about rapid AI advancements and their long-term impacts on established software giants such as Adobe. Meanwhile, investors have also, perhaps to less fanfare, sold off some of the AI hyperscalers to start 2026 as they worry their massive AI capex spending plans aren’t paying off quickly enough. Microsoft, for instance, ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Adobe (ADBE) and Microsoft (MSFT) are trading at their lowest forward earnings multiples in years, presenting potential buying opportunities.
вҖў Adobe's revenue grew 10.5% in fiscal 2025, with AI offerings enhancing its creative software portfolio.
вҖў Microsoft's stock is down 35% from highs due to AI spending and OpenAI partnership concerns, but Azure revenue grew 39% YoY.
вҖў Analysts project strong revenue and EPS growth for both companies, with Adobe's stock potentially offering 40% upside based on its average Zacks price target.
news.Keywords: Software Stocks, Valuation Discount, AI Integration, Earnings Growth, Analyst Targets

news.Title: Got $3,000? 2 Cloud Stocks That Wall Street Analysts Raised Targets on This Month.
news.Content: Cloud computing stocks have been an interesting case this year. Although demand for cloud computing continues to surge, many of the stocks in this industry have sputtered. One of the reasons is the stocks have become too expensive and overvalued after three years of bull market gains. But there are other reasons specific to cloud stocks. Many are dealing with capacity constraints to handle the high demand, which has hurt their ability to maximize profits. Will AI create the world's first ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Arm Holdings (ARM) is launching its own CPU chip, the Arm AGI CPU, for data centers handling agentic AI workloads, with Meta Platforms as a key customer.
вҖў Arm projects the new chips to generate $15 billion annually starting in 2031, significantly increasing total revenue and earnings.
вҖў Needham upgraded Arm to a Buy with a $200 price target, citing its high-stakes bets and transformation for the better.
вҖў Despite a high forward P/E ratio of 61, Arm is seen as a credible AI play due to the rise of agentic AI and the growing role of CPUs in AI data centers.
news.Keywords: CPU Chip, Agentic AI, Data Center Market, Semiconductor Design, Growth Potential

news.Title: York Space and Firefly Are Riding SpaceX's Coattails. Smaller Space Stocks Post Strong Gains.
news.Content: Last Wednesday was a great day to be a space investor. (Thursday and Friday, somewhat less so!) Rumors that SpaceX would file its IPO prospectus , which turned out to be false, set space stocks on fire. Shares of recent space IPOs York Space Systems (NYSE: YSS) and Firefly Aerospace (NASDAQ: FLY) shot up ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў York Space Systems and Firefly Aerospace saw significant stock gains on rumors of SpaceX's IPO filing, but gave back gains afterward.
вҖў SpaceX is rumored to be a $16 billion/year company with about $3 billion in annual profit, significantly larger than York and Firefly.
вҖў York Space Systems has a market cap of $2.7 billion but is losing $84.5 million per year and burning $130 million in negative free cash flow.
вҖў The potential SpaceX IPO could pull investors away from smaller space stocks like York and Firefly.
news.Keywords: Space IPO Rumors, Market Capitalization, Cash Burn, Free Cash Flow, Investor Sentiment

news.Title: Why 1 Top Analyst Says Apple Is a Screaming Buy Right Now
news.Content: Tech is a wreck. So far in 2026, the technology sector is the second-worst-performing sector in the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) , which ranks as the world's second-largest technology company by market cap , is among the laggards. Shares of the iPhone maker are down more than 10% year to date. However, Wall Street remains generally bullish about Apple. The consensus ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring believes Apple (AAPL) is a 'screaming buy,' with a price target suggesting nearly 28% upside.
вҖў A survey indicates a record high number of consumers in the U.S. and China plan to upgrade to new iPhones.
вҖў Apple is expected to be the only major global smartphone vendor gaining market share in 2026.
вҖў Demand for foldable iPhones appears robust, with potential for significant revenue contribution.
news.Keywords: iPhone Upgrades, Market Share Gain, Foldable Phones, Consumer Demand, Analyst Optimism

news.Title: The Nasdaq Is Officially in a Correction. Here's What Investors Should Do, According to History.
news.Content: The Nasdaq-100 index tracks the performance of the top 100 companies (by value) listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange, excluding banks and other financial institutions. A whopping 60% of the index's portfolio is parked in the technology sector alone, so it has a high degree of exposure to rapidly growing artificial intelligence (AI) companies like Nvidia . As a result, the Nasdaq-100 often outperforms more diversified indexes like the S&P 500 , but it also experiences more volatility during periods of market turmoil. The index is currently in correction territory after declining by more than ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The Nasdaq-100 index is in correction territory, down over 10% from its peak, due to spiking oil prices and AI disruption worries.
вҖў Nvidia, the largest holding in the Nasdaq-100 (8.5% weighting), is impacted by broader market downturns.
вҖў Concerns exist about a potential slowdown in AI data center infrastructure spending, particularly regarding OpenAI's commitments.
вҖў History suggests that market corrections can present buying opportunities for patient investors.
news.Keywords: Market Correction, Nasdaq-100, AI Spending, Geopolitical Impact, Investment Opportunity

news.Title: Amazon Stock Investors Just Got Great News Concerning OpenAI and Robotaxis
news.Content: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) shares have fallen 20% from their high due in part to concerns about the company's plan to spend about $200 billion on capital expenditures this year. Also, recession fears have resurfaced because the U.S.-Iran war has pushed oil prices to a multiyear high. That has hurt consumer discretionary stocks like Amazon. However, Amazon shareholders recently got some great news about the company's artificial intelligence and robotaxi businesses. Here are the important details. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Amazon Web Services (AWS) and OpenAI expanded their partnership, with Amazon investing $50 billion and OpenAI committing $138 billion in AWS cloud services over eight years.
вҖў OpenAI will consume about 2 gigawatts of Trainium capacity through AWS, adding credibility to Amazon's custom AI chips.
вҖў AWS will offer a stateful runtime environment (SRE) powered by OpenAI models on Amazon Bedrock, enabling deeper integration than Microsoft Azure's stateless APIs.
вҖў AWS reported 24% revenue growth in Q4, its fastest in over four years, and this partnership could accelerate that growth.
news.Keywords: Cloud Partnership, AI Investment, Custom AI Chips, Stateful Runtime, Revenue Acceleration

news.Title: Is Nvidia a Smart Buy for a Value Investor Right Now?
news.Content: The hottest tech stocks of the times generally aren't on the buy list of value investors -- and this is because they often trade at lofty valuations. Excitement about the prospects of these players prompts investors seeking growth to rush into these names. We've seen this over the past few years with technology stocks focused on artificial intelligence (AI). Companies from Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) to Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) saw their stock prices climb in the triple and quadruple ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia (NVDA) is considered a smart buy for value investors, trading at its lowest level in about a year.
вҖў The company has reported explosive revenue gains (double and triple digits) due to its leading position in AI chips (GPUs).
вҖў Nvidia's GPUs are the fastest, allowing customers to bring AI platforms to market sooner.
вҖў Analysts expect annual revenue to climb 72% in the current full year, making the stock appear undervalued.
news.Keywords: Value Investing, AI Chips, GPU Market, Revenue Growth, Undervalued Stock

news.Title: 2 Beaten-Down AI Stocks to Buy Before the Next Bull Market
news.Content: Although it's still officially in a bull market overall, the artificial intelligence (AI) investment sector may have something to say about that claim. However, once geopolitical tensions stabilize and investors realize that AI spending will last for many more years, some of ...

вҖў Microsoft (MSFT) stock is down over 30% from its highs, presenting a contrarian buy opportunity.

news.Title: The Best Quantum Computing Stock to Buy With $1,000 Right Now
news.Content: While the world fixates on Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) chips fueling today's artificial intelligence (AI) boom, the company is quietly positioning itself as a foundational player in quantum computing. With a modest sum of $1,000 to invest, Nvidia stands out as the smartest quantum AI play -- not because it builds the hardware, but because ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Nvidia is positioning itself as a foundational player in quantum computing by developing software that bridges classical supercomputers and quantum systems.
вҖў The company's CUDA software platform is being extended into quantum toolkits, allowing simulations on Nvidia GPUs that would take years on ordinary computers.
вҖў Nvidia's ecosystem makes quantum experimentation scalable and affordable, offering investors exposure to quantum AI progress beyond raw chip sales.
вҖў The company's data center business generates billions in profit, funding its quantum roadmap, and its ecosystem creates a unique flywheel advantage.
вҖў Investors may not be pricing in ancillary AI-driven growth from quantum simulation royalties, hybrid cloud integrations, and enterprise software licensing.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, AI, software platform, CUDA, hybrid computing

news.Title: Beyond Nvidia: This Under-the-Radar AI Stock Is Up Nearly 300% Over the Past Year
news.Content: Nvidia has been a great investment over the past few years, and I still think it's a great stock pick now. However, many investors are looking for alternatives or new ways to invest in the artificial intelligence (AI) space, and I think I've identified ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Micron (MU) stock has seen significant gains, up nearly 300% over the past year, and is considered a strong investment in the AI space.
вҖў Micron is a critical supplier of High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI computing chips, with the HBM market expected to grow from $35 billion in 2025 to $100 billion by 2028.
вҖў The company currently cannot meet total HBM demand, with capacity only meeting half to two-thirds of demand until 2027.
вҖў Despite Google's TurboQuant algorithm reducing memory demand, it only affects one part of large language models, and overall memory demand is expected to remain strong.
вҖў Micron expects significant revenue growth, projecting $33.5 billion next quarter, up from $23.9 billion this quarter.
news.Keywords: AI, memory, HBM, demand, growth trajectory

news.AI_Summary:
вҖў еҸ°иӮЎз©©еұ…3иҗ¬й»һд№ӢдёҠпјҢж—ҘжҲҗдәӨйҮ‘йЎҚ8000е„„е…ғпјҢдҪҶд»Қжңү1071е®¶е…¬еҸёиӮЎеғ№и·Ңз ҙе№ҙз·ҡпјҢжҜ”йҮҚй«ҳйҒ”50%гҖӮ

вҖў еҸ°иӮЎ31ж—ҘеҸ—зҙҜжҲ°дәӢе»¶зҮ’еҸҠжё…жҳҺйҖЈеҒҮеүҚиіЈеЈ“пјҢзӣӨдёӯеҸҠж”¶зӣӨи·Ңй»һеқҮеҲ—д»Ҡе№ҙз¬¬еӣӣж…ҳзҙҖйҢ„пјҢзөӮе ҙдёӢи·Ң795.17й»һпјҢж”¶еңЁ31722.99й»һгҖӮ

news.Title: Citi Raises Target Price for Amazon.com (AMZN)
news.Content: Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN ) is one of the 10 best stocks that recently issued new debt . On March 25, Citi increased its price target on Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN) from $265 to ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў Citi raised its price target for Amazon.com (AMZN) to $285 from $265, maintaining a Buy rating.
вҖў Estimates for Amazon Web Services (AWS) were raised, driven by AI demand, with predictions of 28% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 and 37% in 2027.
вҖў Wells Fargo maintained an Overweight rating with a $304 price target, highlighting compute capacity as a key success factor for AI-driven demand.
вҖў Hyperscaler capacity is expected to double to 98GW, with annual CapEx reaching $860 billion by 2027, indicating significant cloud industry expansion.
вҖў While Amazon is a strong investment, other AI stocks may offer greater upside potential and less downside risk.
news.Keywords: AWS, AI demand, price target, compute capacity, cloud growth

вҖў еҮұеҹәеҸ°зҒЈTOP50(009816)гҖҒдё»еӢ•зөұдёҖеҸ°иӮЎеўһй•·(00981A)гҖҒзҫӨзӣҠеҸ°зҒЈзІҫйҒёй«ҳжҒҜ(00919)гҖҒе…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ50(0050)еҸҠе…ғеӨ§еҸ°зҒЈ50еҸҚ1(00632R)зӮә3жңҲжҲҗдәӨйҮҸеүҚдә”еҗҚгҖӮ

news.Title: Is the AI Bubble Bursting? Here Are 2 Top Stocks to Buy the Dip on When It Does.
news.Content: Artificial intelligence (AI) companies have driven the stock market higher in the past few years. Now, they may be contributing to it returning to Earth. Rapidly growing AI entities like OpenAI are scaling back commitments to chip spending and canceling projects such as video generator Sora, which could reduce overall chip spending. Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
вҖў The AI bubble may be bursting, with companies like OpenAI scaling back chip spending, potentially leading to a stock market bear market.
вҖў This downturn presents a buying opportunity for long-term investors to acquire stocks at discounted prices.
вҖў Airbnb (ABNB) is mentioned as a potential dip-buy, with consistent market share gains and 11% revenue growth last quarter.
вҖў Interactive Brokers (IBKR) is also highlighted as a potential dip-buy, with 4.65 million active accounts and 31% year-over-year growth.
вҖў Both ABNB and IBKR are seen as potentially attractive at lower prices if the AI bubble pops.
news.Keywords: AI bubble, bear market, dip-buying, tech stocks, market downturn

вҖў еүҚеңӢжі°жҠ•дҝЎи‘ЈдәӢй•·ејөйҢ«жҢҮеҮәпјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»жҳҺе№ҙдёҠзңӢ3,000е…ғпјҢй җдј°EPS 125е…ғпјҢд»Ҙ25еҖҚжң¬зӣҠжҜ”дҫҶзңӢпјҢиӮЎеғ№3еӯ—й ӯжҳҜеҗҲзҗҶзҡ„гҖӮ
вҖў д»–иӘҚзӮәпјҢеҸ°иӮЎеӣ е…·еӮҷAIеүӣжҖ§йңҖжұӮеј·еӢҒгҖҒеҸ°зҒЈдҪҚиҷ•AIйҮҚиҰҒдҫӣжҮүйҸҲзӯүеҹәжң¬йқўе„ӘеӢўпјҢжҳҺе№ҙеӨ§зӣӨжңүжңӣз«ҷдёҠ4иҗ¬й»һгҖӮ
вҖў ејөйҢ«е»әиӯ°пјҢеҸ°з©Қйӣ»и·ҢеҲ°1,800е…ғд»ҘдёӢеҸҜй–үзңјиІ·пјҢд»Ҡе№ҙеҸ°иӮЎиө°еӢўиҲҮеҺ»е№ҙжҮүжңғдёҖжЁЈпјҢз¬¬дёҖеӯЈдҝ®жӯЈгҖҒз¬¬еӣӣеӯЈеҶҚиЎқдёҠдҫҶгҖӮ
вҖў зӣ®еүҚиҰҒж“”еҝғзҡ„жҳҜйҖҡиҶЁпјҢзҫҺеңӢд»Ҡе№ҙдёҚиҮіж–јжңғеҚҮжҒҜпјҢдҪҶйҷҚжҒҜж©ҹжңғйҷҚдҪҺгҖӮ

вҖў дјҠжң—ж”»ж“ҠдёӯжқұйҳҝжӢүдјҜеңӢе®¶ж°‘з”ҹиЁӯж–ҪпјҢеҲәжҝҖиҘҝеҫ·е·һеҺҹжІ№еғ№ж јеҶҚеүө106зҫҺе…ғй«ҳй»һпјҢзҫҺиӮЎиІ»еҚҠжҢҮж•ёеӨ§и·Ң4.23%пјҢеҸ°иӮЎд№ҹи·ҹи‘—еҠ еӨ§дёӢи·ҢгҖӮ
